data.title: Galaxy Digital Transfers Significant Bitcoin Holdings to Exchanges
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Arkham has reported that Galaxy Digital transferred 3,420 bitcoins, valued at approximately $395 million, to various cryptocurrency exchanges within a 20-minute timeframe. Additionally, 250 bitcoins were sent to an unidentified address.

Earlier reports indicated that on July 18, a Bitcoin whale from the Satoshi era transferred the last of their 40,192 BTC, worth around $4.83 billion, to Galaxy Digital. Over the past four days, this Bitcoin OG has moved a total of 80,201 BTC, valued at approximately $9.6 billion, to Galaxy Digital.

data.title: South Korean Bank Seeks Trademark for Dollar and Yen-Linked Stablecoins
data.description: According to PANews, South Korea's KB Kookmin Bank has filed a trademark application for stablecoins linked to the U.S. dollar and Japanese yen. The application, submitted on July 22, includes 27 trademark combinations that integrate the bank's brand 'KB' with currency symbols such as USD and JPY, alongside stablecoin indicators like 'ST', 'S', and 'C'. Examples of these combinations include 'USDKB', 'JPYKB', 'USDST', 'USDC', 'JPYH', and 'JPYONE'. A representative from KB Kookmin Bank stated that the registration is a proactive measure to secure trademarks in the evolving financial landscape.

data.description:

data.title: Michael Saylor’s Strategy upsizes ‘stretch’ preferred stock sale to $2.8 billion
data.description:
Michael Saylor’s Strategy launched and upsized a new preferred stock offering from $500M to $2. 8 billion. The ‘Stretch’ security promises a hefty 9% annual payout with no end date and a flexible, adjustable dividend. The deal is the latest in Saylor’s years-long effort to transform Strategy into a financial vehicle to acquire Bitcoin
source: https://coinjournal.net/news/michael-saylors-strategy-upsizes-stretch-preferred-stock-sale-to-2-8-billion/

data.title: Ethereum Purchase Results in Significant Losses
data.description: According to PANews, a blockchain analyst known as @ai_9684xtpa reported that eight hours ago, an address identified as 0x208...5b971 spent 6.98 million USDC to acquire 1,862 ETH at an average price of $3,750.63. The transaction has resulted in a current unrealized loss of $237,000.

data.title: Bitcoin Cycle Theory Deemed Ineffective by CryptoQuant CEO
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, CryptoQuant founder and CEO Ki Young Ju recently expressed on the X platform that the Bitcoin cycle theory is no longer valid. He explained that his previous predictions were based on this theory, which suggested buying when whales accumulate and selling when retail investors enter the market. However, this pattern has now changed.

In the last cycle, whales sold their holdings to retail investors. This time, older whales have sold to new long-term holders. The scale of institutional investment has exceeded expectations, rendering trading less significant as the number of holders now surpasses traders. Ju admitted to overlooking this structural change when assessing the end of the bull market.
